title: 7 bit wide memory is stored as 8 bit array, but is not masked to 7 bit on read
body:
Run:

    verilator --cc ram_8x7.sv

This generates code for R0_data below, but this read is not masked to the bitwidth of R0_data:

```c++
    vlSelfRef.R0_data = ((IData)(vlSelfRef.R0_en) ? 
                         vlSelfRef.ram_8x7__DOT__Memory
                         [vlSelfRef.R0_addr] : 0U);
```


ram_8x7.sv:

```verilog
module ram_8x7(
  input  [2:0] R0_addr,
  input        R0_en,
               R0_clk,
  output [6:0] R0_data,
  input  [2:0] W0_addr,
  input        W0_en,
               W0_clk,
  input  [6:0] W0_data
);

  reg [6:0] Memory[0:7];
  always @(posedge W0_clk) begin
    if (W0_en)
      Memory[W0_addr] <= W0_data;
  end // always @(posedge)
  assign R0_data = R0_en ? Memory[R0_addr] : 7'bx;
endmodule
```


    $ verilator --version
    Verilator 5.032 2025-01-01 rev (Debian 5.032-1)
